title: Green Chilli Crumbly Canadian Garlic Biryani
description: A festive garlicy and crumbly Canadian dish infused with green chilli, perfect for a dash-friendly meal that can be air-fried to reduce mess.
instructions: Preheat your air fryer to 350°F (175°C). Rinse the basmati rice and soak it in water for at least an hour.
Heat ghee or oil in a pan, add cumin seeds and let them splutter. Add chopped green chillies and garlic, sauté until fragrant.
Drain and rinse the soaked rice. Add to the pan with salt, mix well, ensuring all grains are coated with spices.
Cook over medium heat for about 10 minutes or until water is absorbed. Cover partially and let it rest for at least 3 minutes before fluffing up with a fork.

Transfer the cooked rice mixture into an air fryer basket, press down slightly to make it crumbly if desired, then cook in your preheated air fryer for about 5-7 minutes or until golden brown and crispy on top.
Serve hot as a snack or side dish.
